commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=0d6ff017fd21ecfd52fa90d08eb1ee5228631ad7)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Kubernetes docs and a deployment manifest `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=1536` default to reduce OOM kills under pod memory limits.

PR surface: Docs +10, Other +6. Total +16 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding: current Node source shows cgroup-aware V8 default configuration, contradicting the new docs/manifest wording. The original Kubernetes OOM report is only contributor-reported and was not independently reproduced in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs contributor-side real behavior proof plus maintainer review of the Kubernetes deployment-default tradeoff; it should not enter an automated repair lane while proof is missing.

target.title: typedocs-only PR to help others avoid kubernetes OOM-kills
target.body:
docs(k8s) OOM-kills happen when openclaw runs its various node tools when k8s limits are set.

## What Problem This Solves

on kubernetes, OOM-kills happen when openclaw runs its various node tools when k8s limits are set.

## Why This Change Was Made

This documentation and example would have saved me the time it took to debug and discover the node setting needed to stop node from trying to gobble up more resources than allowed.

The crash is especially hard to recover from because upon automatic restart the agent goes into a blocking unresponsive state with no logging due to the gateway lock loop state the OOM-kill puts the container into - also observed in the fly fix https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/1572

## User Impact

The impact is avoiding the fatal crash that happens when the agent runs its node-based tools for purposes such as status/healthcheck.

## Evidence

1. The change in the example deployment.yaml fixed the issue for me locally.
2. Fix is modeled after the existing openclaw fly docs and examples as fly container orchestration has the same exposure to this issue and solved it specific for fly.  https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/1572
- deploy/fly.private.toml:22 — NODE_OPTIONS = "--max-old-space-size=1536" (the actual config)
- docs/install/fly.md:59 — same line, in the Fly install doc
target.author: navicore
